figures.caption: Table 1: Values and units used in the numerical simulation of the propagation of a Marshak-type wave in an opaque medium, reference case.
Figure 2: Actual architecture of the neural network.

Figure 4: Execution result after niter = 10k iterations using the Adam optimizer (learning rate and other parameters are PyTorch defaults). Left: the Marshak profiles at different time instances. Right: the complete T̃ evolution, time is the ordinate. We note that the t, x space is clearly separated in three regions: ’hot’ (yellow), ’wave’ (red) and ’cold’ (blue).
Figure 7: Same as Figure 4 except that in each case we take out a single ingredient. Left: we use ’linear’ activation; the profiles are clearly not correct; Right: no PDE (i.e. cϕ = 0).
